blocks.content: Mr. Alvarez topped off both their cups. Then he set the carafe down and looked at Ethan with the expression he reserved for things that required both precision and care.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: Before you do anything else — before the ring, before the question — there is one conversation you must have first.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: Thomas Hailey has been watching his daughter for twenty-two years. He has watched everyone she brings close, and he has watched them carefully, the way a man watches when he knows that whoever loves his child will one day be in a room with the weight of things that cannot be fixed. He has a very good sense of who is capable of that room and who is not.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: He has already seen you. He has been seeing you. Go to him. Look him in the eye. Tell him what you told me just now — plainly, the way you said it. Don't prepare a speech. Just tell him the truth.
He stood, picked up the carafe, and paused at the edge of the table.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: But do it right. She deserves a man who does it right.
He walked back toward the counter. Ethan sat with his coffee. Then he pulled out his phone and typed a name he had never called before.
